title: crataegus oxyacantha
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha, also known as the hawthorn tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree with thorny branches and clusters of small, white flowers. The medicinal properties of Crataegus oxyacantha lie in its ability to support cardiovascular health, lower blood pressure, and improve circulation. The health benefits of hawthorn include reducing the risk of heart failure, improving blood lipid profiles, and alleviating symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. Hawthorn is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and can also be used as a dried flower or berry in herbal supplements and food products.
intro_study: According to Farmaco (Societa chimica italiana : 1989), Crataegus oxyacantha contains flavonoids such as luteolin, luteolin-3', 7-diglucoside, apigenin, apigenin-7-O-glucoside and rutin, which may have medicinal properties but their specific medicinal purposes are not mentioned in this study.
benefits: Crataegus oxyacantha is used to promote cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, which can lead to a lower risk of heart disease and stroke. This plant is also used to boost the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression. Its health benefits extend to anti-aging and skin health, as it has been shown to reduce wrinkles, fine lines, and age spots. Additionally, Crataegus oxyacantha has been used to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including irritable bowel syndrome, diarrhea, and stomach ulcers.
actions: Crataegus oxyacantha has been found to exhibit various therapeutic actions, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, cardiotonic, and vasodilatory properties, which affect the cardiovascular system, nervous system, and circulatory system. Additionally, it possesses antioxidant, hepatoprotective, and free radical-scavenging properties, influencing the liver and immune system. The plant also exhibits diuretic, hypotensive, and vasorelaxant actions, impacting the urinary system and vascular system. Furthermore, it has been shown to have adaptogenic, anxiolytic, and sedative effects, influencing the nervous system and endocrine system.
constituents: Crataegus oxyacantha has been found to contain an array of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Tannins, like epicatechin and catechin, present in the plant, have been shown to possess antimicrobial and astringent properties, while also contributing to its antioxidant activity. The plant also contains phenolic acids, like caffeic and ferulic acid, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Additionally, triterpenoids, including ursolic and oleanolic acid, have been identified in Crataegus oxyacantha, displaying anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
parts: The most commonly used parts of Crataegus oxyacantha are the flowers, fruits, leaves, and bark. The flowers of the Crataegus oxyacantha plant are used to create teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to aid in lowering blood pressure and preventing cardiovascular disease. The fruits, often referred to as hawthorn berries, are used in supplements, jams, and wines, and are thought to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the risk of heart disease and improve cognitive function. The leaves and bark of the plant are used to create teas and infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, helping to soothe digestive issues and reduce the severity of colds and flu.
preparations: Crataegus oxyacantha, also known as hawthorn, is a flowering plant used in traditional medicine for its cardiovascular benefits. The most common herbal preparations of Crataegus oxyacantha for health purposes include infusions, tinctures, and capsules. Infusions of hawthorn flowers and leaves are used to treat high blood pressure, heart failure, and angina by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation, while tinctures are used to support cardiovascular health and reduce anxiety. Capsules containing hawthorn extract are also used to lower cholesterol levels, improve exercise performance, and alleviate symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats.
side_effects: Crataegus oxyacantha side effects are typically associated with individuals taking high doses or for extended periods. Some common side effects include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. Additionally, individuals taking Crataegus oxyacantha may experience dizziness, headaches, and fatigue, which are often more pronounced in elderly or sensitive individuals. Precautions include being cautious with blood-thinning medications and monitoring blood pressure, as excessive consumption may lead to interactions and adverse effects such as bruising, bleeding, and orthostatic hypotension.
benefits_desc: Crataegus oxyacantha is used to treat a variety of health issues, including high blood pressure, heart failure, and chest pain. It has been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow, which can lead to a decrease in the risk of heart disease and stroke. This plant has also been found to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and promote overall health and well-being. Additionally, Crataegus oxyacantha has been used to treat conditions such as diabetes, obesity, and certain types of cancer, as well as to improve cognitive function and boost the immune system.

actions_desc: Crataegus oxyacantha has demonstrated anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, exerting its effects on the cardiovascular, immune, and nervous systems. Its flavonoids and polyphenols have been shown to exhibit cardiotonic, vasodilatory, and anti-arrhythmic actions, impacting the cardiovascular system's functioning. Additionally, the plant's therapeutic actions include anti-hypertensive, anxiolytic, and sedative effects, influencing the nervous system's response to stress and promoting relaxation. The plant's bioactive compounds also exhibit anti-oxidative, hepatoprotective, and immunomodulatory actions, affecting the digestive, hepatic, and immune systems.

constituents_desc: Crataegus oxyacantha has been found to be rich in a variety of bioactive constituents, including flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol, which possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect cells from damage. The plant also contains tannins, like gallic acid and ellagic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. Furthermore, Crataegus oxyacantha is a source of phenolic acids, including caffeic acid, ferulic acid, and sinapic acid, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects. Additionally, the plant is rich in saponins, like aesculetin and scopoletin, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, as well as cardiotonic compounds like crataegus saponins, which have been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health.
parts_desc: The most commonly used parts of Crataegus oxyacantha are the berries, leaves, flowers, and bark. The berries of the plant are rich in antioxidants and are used to treat various health issues, such as high blood pressure, heart failure, and inflammation. The leaves are used to make teas and infusions that help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The flowers and bark are used in traditional medicine to treat conditions like diarrhea, dysentery, and to boost the immune system, with the flowers also being used to treat anxiety and insomnia.

preparations_desc: Crataegus oxyacantha, also known as the hawthorn tree, is a popular herbal remedy used to promote cardiovascular health. The most common herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, tinctures, and capsules. Infusions and decoctions are often used to treat mild heart conditions, such as high blood pressure and angina, while tinctures and capsules are commonly used to support cardiovascular health, improve circulation, and reduce the risk of heart failure. Additionally, hawthorn preparations have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, improve digestion, and boost overall cardiovascular function.

side_effects_desc: Crataegus oxyacantha side effects are numerous and can be concerning for those taking it, particularly the elderly and individuals with pre-existing medical conditions. Side effects can include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach pain, as well as cardiovascular problems like palpitations, irregular heartbeat, and low blood pressure. Additionally, users may experience dizziness, fatigue, headaches, and allergic reactions like hives and itching. Precautions are advised for those taking medications for diabetes, high blood pressure, or heart conditions, as interactions with Crataegus oxyacantha may exacerbate these conditions.

side_effects_list.name: allergic reactions
side_effects_list.desc: some individuals may experience allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing, after consuming crataegus oxyacantha.

side_effects_list.name: digestive issues
side_effects_list.desc: crataegus oxyacantha may cause digestive issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, especially when taken in high doses.

side_effects_list.name: interactions with medications
side_effects_list.desc: this plant may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, diabetes medications, and blood pressure medications, which can lead to adverse effects.

side_effects_list.name: lightheadedness and dizziness
side_effects_list.desc: consuming crataegus oxyacantha may cause lightheadedness and dizziness, especially when standing up quickly or engaging in physical activity.

side_effects_list.desc: some individuals may experience skin rashes or itching after consuming crataegus oxyacantha.

side_effects_list.desc: crataegus oxyacantha may cause stomach pain or discomfort, especially when taken in high doses or in combination with other medications.

side_effects_list.name: vasodilation
side_effects_list.desc: the plant may cause vasodilation, leading to low blood pressure, which can be problematic for individuals with pre-existing hypotension or cardiovascular conditions.
